NKJV.1: When the Day of Pentecost had fully come, they were all with one accord in one place.
NKJV.2: And suddenly there came a sound from heaven, as of a rushing mighty wind, and it filled the whole house where they were sitting.
NKJV.3: Then there appeared to them divided tongues, as of fire, and one sat upon each of them.
NKJV.4: And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance.
NKJV.5: And there were dwelling in Jerusalem Jews, devout men, from every nation under heaven.
NKJV.6: And when this sound occurred, the multitude came together, and were confused, because everyone heard them speak in his own language.
NKJV.7: Then they were all amazed and marveled, saying to one another, “Look, are not all these who speak Galileans?
NKJV.8: And how is it that we hear, each in our own language in which we were born?
NKJV.9: Parthians and Medes and Elamites, those dwelling in Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,
NKJV.10: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of Libya adjoining Cyrene, visitors from Rome, both Jews and proselytes,
NKJV.11: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them speaking in our own tongues the wonderful works of God.”
NKJV.12: So they were all amazed and perplexed, saying to one another, “Whatever could this mean?”
NKJV.13: Others mocking said, “They are full of new wine.”
NKJV.14: But Peter, standing up with the eleven, raised his voice and said to them, “Men of Judea and all who dwell in Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and heed my words.
NKJV.15: For these are not drunk, as you suppose, since it is only the third hour of the day.
NKJV.16: But this is what was spoken by the prophet Joel:
NKJV.17: ‘And  it shall come to pass in the last days, says God,  That I will pour out of My Spirit on all flesh; Your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, Your young men shall see visions, Your old men shall dream dreams.
NKJV.18: And on My menservants and on My maidservants I will pour out My Spirit in those days;  And they shall prophesy.
NKJV.19: I will show wonders in heaven above And signs in the earth beneath: Blood and fire and vapor of smoke.
NKJV.20: The sun shall be turned into darkness, And the moon into blood, Before the coming of the great and awesome day of the LORD.
NKJV.21: And it shall come to pass That whoever calls on the name of the LORD Shall be saved.’
NKJV.22: “Men of Israel, hear these words: Jesus of Nazareth, a Man attested by God to you by miracles, wonders, and signs which God did through Him in your midst, as you yourselves also know—
NKJV.23: Him, being delivered by the determined purpose and foreknowledge of God, you have taken by lawless hands, have crucified, and put to death;
NKJV.24: whom God raised up, having loosed the pains of death, because it was not possible that He should be held by it.
NKJV.25: For David says concerning Him: ‘I foresaw the LORD always before my face, For He is at my right hand, that I may not be shaken.
NKJV.26: Therefore my heart rejoiced, and my tongue was glad; Moreover my flesh also will rest in hope.
NKJV.27: For You will not leave my soul in Hades, Nor will You allow Your Holy One to see corruption.
NKJV.28: You have made known to me the ways of life; You will make me full of joy in Your presence.’
NKJV.29: “Men and brethren, let me speak freely to you of the patriarch David, that he is both dead and buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.
NKJV.30: Therefore, being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him that of the fruit of his body, according to the flesh, He would raise up the Christ to sit on his throne,
NKJV.31: he, foreseeing this, spoke concerning the resurrection of the Christ, that His soul was not left in Hades, nor did His flesh see corruption.
NKJV.32: This Jesus God has raised up, of which we are all witnesses.
NKJV.33: Therefore being exalted to the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, He poured out this which you now see and hear.
NKJV.34: “For David did not ascend into the heavens, but he says himself: ‘The LORD said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand,
NKJV.35: Till I make Your enemies Your footstool.” ’
NKJV.36: “Therefore let all the house of Israel know assuredly that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.”
NKJV.37: Now when they heard this, they were cut to the heart, and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, “Men and brethren, what shall we do?”
NKJV.38: Then Peter said to them, “Repent, and let every one of you be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins; and you shall receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.39: For the promise is to you and to your children, and to all who are afar off, as many as the Lord our God will call.”
NKJV.40: And with many other words he testified and exhorted them, saying, “Be saved from this perverse generation.”
NKJV.41: Then those who gladly received his word were baptized; and that day about three thousand souls were added to them.
NKJV.42: And they continued steadfastly in the apostles’ doctrine and fellowship, in the breaking of bread, and in prayers.
NKJV.43: Then fear came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were done through the apostles.
NKJV.44: Now all who believed were together, and had all things in common,
NKJV.45: and sold their possessions and goods, and divided them among all, as anyone had need.
NKJV.46: So continuing daily with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread from house to house, they ate their food with gladness and simplicity of heart,
NKJV.47: praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord added to the church daily those who were being saved.

ESV.1: When the day of Pentecost arrived, they were all together in one place.
ESV.2: And suddenly there came from heaven a sound like a mighty rushing wind, and it filled the entire house where they were sitting.
ESV.3: And divided tongues as of fire appeared to them and rested  on each one of them.
ESV.4: And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other tongues as the Spirit gave them utterance.
ESV.5: Now there were dwelling in Jerusalem Jews, devout men from every nation under heaven.
ESV.6: And at this sound the multitude came together, and they were bewildered, because each one was hearing them speak in his own language.
ESV.7: And they were amazed and astonished, saying, “Are not all these who are speaking Galileans?
ESV.8: And how is it that we hear, each of us in his own native language?
ESV.9: Parthians and Medes and Elamites and residents of Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,
ESV.10: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of Libya belonging to Cyrene, and visitors from Rome,
ESV.11: both Jews and proselytes, Cretans and Arabians — we hear them telling in our own tongues the mighty works of God.”
ESV.12: And all were amazed and perplexed, saying to one another, “What does this mean?”
ESV.13: But others mocking said, “They are filled with new wine.”
ESV.14: But Peter, standing with the eleven, lifted up his voice and addressed them: “Men of Judea and all who dwell in Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and give ear to my words.
ESV.15: For these people are not drunk, as you suppose, since it is only the third hour of the day.
ESV.16: But this is what was uttered through the prophet Joel:
ESV.17: “‘And in the last days it shall be, God declares, that I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh, and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams;
ESV.18: even on my male servants and female servants in those days I will pour out my Spirit, and they shall prophesy.
ESV.19: And I will show wonders in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood, and fire, and vapor of smoke;
ESV.20: the sun shall be turned to darkness and the moon to blood, before the day of the Lord comes, the great and magnificent day.
ESV.21: And it shall come to pass that everyone who calls upon the name of the Lord shall be saved.’
ESV.22: “Men of Israel, hear these words: Jesus of Nazareth, a man attested to you by God with mighty works and wonders and signs that God did through him in your midst, as you yourselves know —
ESV.23: this Jesus,  delivered up according to the definite plan and foreknowledge of God, you crucified and killed by the hands of lawless men.
ESV.24: God raised him up, loosing the pangs of death, because it was not possible for him to be held by it.
ESV.25: For David says concerning him, “‘I saw the Lord always before me, for he is at my right hand that I may not be shaken;
ESV.26: therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced; my flesh also will dwell in hope.
ESV.27: For you will not abandon my soul to Hades, or let your Holy One see corruption.
ESV.28: You have made known to me the paths of life; you will make me full of gladness with your presence.’
ESV.29: “Brothers, I may say to you with confidence about the patriarch David that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.
ESV.30: Being therefore a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him that he would set one of his descendants on his throne,
ESV.31: he foresaw and spoke about the resurrection of the Christ, that he was not abandoned to Hades, nor did his flesh see corruption.
ESV.32: This Jesus God raised up, and of that we all are witnesses.
ESV.33: Being therefore exalted at the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this that you yourselves are seeing and hearing.
ESV.34: For David did not ascend into the heavens, but he himself says, “‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,
ESV.35: until I make your enemies your footstool.”’
ESV.36: Let all the house of Israel therefore know for certain that God has made him both Lord and Christ, this Jesus whom you crucified.”
ESV.37: Now when they heard this they were cut to the heart, and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, “Brothers, what shall we do?”
ESV.38: And Peter said to them, “Repent and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
ESV.39: For the promise is for you and for your children and for all who are far off, everyone whom the Lord our God calls to himself.”
ESV.40: And with many other words he bore witness and continued to exhort them, saying, “Save yourselves from this crooked generation.”
ESV.41: So those who received his word were baptized, and there were added that day about three thousand souls.
ESV.42: And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers.
ESV.43: And awe  came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles.
ESV.44: And all who believed were together and had all things in common.
ESV.45: And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need.
ESV.46: And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts,
ESV.47: praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord added to their number day by day those who were being saved.

NASB.1: When the day of Pentecost had come, they were all together in one place.
NASB.2: And suddenly there came from heaven a noise like a violent rushing wind, and it filled the whole house where they were sitting.
NASB.3: And there appeared to them tongues as of fire distributing themselves, and they rested on each one of them.
NASB.4: And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit was giving them utterance.
NASB.5: Now there were Jews living in Jerusalem, devout men from every nation under heaven.
NASB.6: And when this sound occurred, the crowd came together, and were bewildered because each one of them was hearing them speak in his own language.
NASB.7: They were amazed and astonished, saying, 'Why, are not all these who are speaking Galileans?
NASB.8: 'And how is it that we each hear [them] in our own language to which we were born?
NASB.9: 'Parthians and Medes and Elamites, and residents of Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,
NASB.10: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the districts of Libya around Cyrene, and visitors from Rome, both Jews and proselytes,
NASB.11: Cretans and Arabs-- we hear them in our [own] tongues speaking of the mighty deeds of God.'
NASB.12: And they all continued in amazement and great perplexity, saying to one another, 'What does this mean?'
NASB.13: But others were mocking and saying, 'They are full of sweet wine.'
NASB.14: But Peter, taking his stand with the eleven, raised his voice and declared to them: 'Men of Judea and all you who live in Jerusalem, let this be known to you and give heed to my words.
NASB.15: 'For these men are not drunk, as you suppose, for it is [only] the third hour of the day;
NASB.16: but this is what was spoken of through the prophet Joel:
NASB.17: 'AND IT SHALL BE IN THE LAST DAYS,' God says, 'THAT I WILL POUR FORTH OF MY SPIRIT ON ALL MANKIND; AND YOUR SONS AND YOUR DAUGHTERS SHALL PROPHESY, AND YOUR YOUNG MEN SHALL SEE VISIONS, AND YOUR OLD MEN SHALL DREAM DREAMS;
NASB.18: EVEN ON MY BONDSLAVES, BOTH MEN AND WOMEN, I WILL IN THOSE DAYS POUR FORTH OF MY SPIRIT And they shall prophesy.
NASB.19: 'AND I WILL GRANT WONDERS IN THE SKY ABOVE AND SIGNS ON THE EARTH BELOW, BLOOD, AND FIRE, AND VAPOR OF SMOKE.
NASB.20: 'THE SUN WILL BE TURNED INTO DARKNESS AND THE MOON INTO BLOOD, BEFORE THE GREAT AND GLORIOUS DAY OF THE LORD SHALL COME.
NASB.21: 'AND IT SHALL BE THAT EVERYONE WHO CALLS ON THE NAME OF THE LORD WILL BE SAVED.'
NASB.22: 'Men of Israel, listen to these words: Jesus the Nazarene, a man attested to you by God with miracles and wonders and signs which God performed through Him in your midst, just as you yourselves know--
NASB.23: this [Man], delivered over by the predetermined plan and foreknowledge of God, you nailed to a cross by the hands of godless men and put [Him] to death.
NASB.24: 'But God raised Him up again, putting an end to the agony of death, since it was impossible for Him to be held in its power.
NASB.25: 'For David says of Him, 'I SAW THE LORD ALWAYS IN MY PRESENCE; FOR HE IS AT MY RIGHT HAND, SO THAT I WILL NOT BE SHAKEN.
NASB.26: 'THEREFORE MY HEART WAS GLAD AND MY TONGUE EXULTED; MOREOVER MY FLESH ALSO WILL LIVE IN HOPE;
NASB.27: BECAUSE YOU WILL NOT ABANDON MY SOUL TO HADES, NOR ALLOW YOUR HOLY ONE TO UNDERGO DECAY.
NASB.28: 'YOU HAVE MADE KNOWN TO ME THE WAYS OF LIFE; YOU WILL MAKE ME FULL OF GLADNESS WITH YOUR PRESENCE.'
NASB.29: 'Brethren, I may confidently say to you regarding the patriarch David that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.
NASB.30: 'And so, because he was a prophet and knew that GOD HAD SWORN TO HIM WITH AN OATH TO SEAT [one] OF HIS DESCENDANTS ON HIS THRONE,
NASB.31: he looked ahead and spoke of the resurrection of the Christ, that HE WAS NEITHER ABANDONED TO HADES, NOR DID His flesh SUFFER DECAY.
NASB.32: 'This Jesus God raised up again, to which we are all witnesses.
NASB.33: 'Therefore having been exalted to the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, He has poured forth this which you both see and hear.
NASB.34: 'For it was not David who ascended into heaven, but he himself says: 'THE LORD SAID TO MY LORD, 'SIT AT MY RIGHT HAND,
NASB.35: UNTIL I MAKE YOUR ENEMIES A FOOTSTOOL FOR YOUR FEET.''
NASB.36: 'Therefore let all the house of Israel know for certain that God has made Him both Lord and Christ-- this Jesus whom you crucified.'
NASB.37: Now when they heard [this], they were pierced to the heart, and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, 'Brethren, what shall we do?'
NASB.38: Peter [said] to them, 'Repent, and each of you be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins; and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
NASB.39: 'For the promise is for you and your children and for all who are far off, as many as the Lord our God will call to Himself.'
NASB.40: And with many other words he solemnly testified and kept on exhorting them, saying, 'Be saved from this perverse generation!'
NASB.41: So then, those who had received his word were baptized; and that day there were added about three thousand souls.
NASB.42: They were continually devoting themselves to the apostles' teaching and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer.
NASB.43: Everyone kept feeling a sense of awe; and many wonders and signs were taking place through the apostles.
NASB.44: And all those who had believed were together and had all things in common;
NASB.45: and they [began] selling their property and possessions and were sharing them with all, as anyone might have need.
NASB.46: Day by day continuing with one mind in the temple, and breaking bread from house to house, they were taking their meals together with gladness and sincerity of heart,
NASB.47: praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord was adding to their number day by day those who were being saved.

NLT.1: On the day of Pentecost all the believers were meeting together in one place.
NLT.2: Suddenly, there was a sound from heaven like the roaring of a mighty windstorm, and it filled the house where they were sitting.
NLT.3: Then, what looked like flames or tongues of fire appeared and settled on each of them.
NLT.4: And everyone present was filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking in other languages, as the Holy Spirit gave them this ability.
NLT.5: At that time there were devout Jews from every nation living in Jerusalem.
NLT.6: When they heard the loud noise, everyone came running, and they were bewildered to hear their own languages being spoken by the believers.
NLT.7: They were completely amazed. “How can this be?” they exclaimed. “These people are all from Galilee,
NLT.8: and yet we hear them speaking in our own native languages!
NLT.9: Here we are — Parthians, Medes, Elamites, people from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, the province of Asia,
NLT.10: Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, and the areas of Libya around Cyrene, visitors from Rome
NLT.11: (both Jews and converts to Judaism), Cretans, and Arabs. And we all hear these people speaking in our own languages about the wonderful things God has done!”
NLT.12: They stood there amazed and perplexed. “What can this mean?” they asked each other.
NLT.13: But others in the crowd ridiculed them, saying, “They’re just drunk, that’s all!”
NLT.14: Then Peter stepped forward with the eleven other apostles and shouted to the crowd, “Listen carefully, all of you, fellow Jews and residents of Jerusalem! Make no mistake about this.
NLT.15: These people are not drunk, as some of you are assuming. Nine o’clock in the morning is much too early for that.
NLT.16: No, what you see was predicted long ago by the prophet Joel:
NLT.17:
‘In the last days,’ God says,
‘I will pour out my Spirit upon all people.
Your sons and daughters will prophesy.
Your young men will see visions,
and your old men will dream dreams.
NLT.18:
In those days I will pour out my Spirit
even on my servants — men and women alike —
and they will prophesy.
NLT.19:
And I will cause wonders in the heavens above
and signs on the earth below —
blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
NLT.20:
The sun will become dark,
and the moon will turn blood red
before that great and glorious day of the LORD arrives.
NLT.21:
But everyone who calls on the name of the LORD
will be saved.’
NLT.22: “People of Israel, listen! God publicly endorsed Jesus the Nazarene by doing powerful miracles, wonders, and signs through him, as you well know.
NLT.23: But God knew what would happen, and his prearranged plan was carried out when Jesus was betrayed. With the help of lawless Gentiles, you nailed him to a cross and killed him.
NLT.24: But God released him from the horrors of death and raised him back to life, for death could not keep him in its grip.
NLT.25:
King David said this about him: ‘I see that the LORD is always with me.
I will not be shaken, for he is right beside me.
NLT.26:
No wonder my heart is glad,
and my tongue shouts his praises!
My body rests in hope.
NLT.27:
For you will not leave my soul among the dead
or allow your Holy One to rot in the grave.
NLT.28:
You have shown me the way of life,
and you will fill me with the joy of your presence.’
NLT.29: “Dear brothers, think about this! You can be sure that the patriarch David wasn’t referring to himself, for he died and was buried, and his tomb is still here among us.
NLT.30: But he was a prophet, and he knew God had promised with an oath that one of David’s own descendants would sit on his throne.
NLT.31: David was looking into the future and speaking of the Messiah’s resurrection. He was saying that God would not leave him among the dead or allow his body to rot in the grave.
NLT.32: “God raised Jesus from the dead, and we are all witnesses of this.
NLT.33: Now he is exalted to the place of highest honor in heaven, at God’s right hand. And the Father, as he had promised, gave him the Holy Spirit to pour out upon us, just as you see and hear today.
NLT.34:
For David himself never ascended into heaven, yet he said, ‘The LORD said to my Lord,
“Sit in the place of honor at my right hand
NLT.35:
until I humble your enemies,
making them a footstool under your feet.”’
NLT.36: “So let everyone in Israel know for certain that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, to be both Lord and Messiah!”
NLT.37: Peter’s words pierced their hearts, and they said to him and to the other apostles, “Brothers, what should we do?”
NLT.38: Peter replied, “Each of you must repent of your sins and turn to God, and be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
NLT.39: This promise is to you, to your children, and to those far away — all who have been called by the Lord our God.”
NLT.40: Then Peter continued preaching for a long time, strongly urging all his listeners, “Save yourselves from this crooked generation!”
NLT.41: Those who believed what Peter said were baptized and added to the church that day — about 3,000 in all.
NLT.42: All the believers devoted themselves to the apostles’ teaching, and to fellowship, and to sharing in meals (including the Lord’s Supper ), and to prayer.
NLT.43: A deep sense of awe came over them all, and the apostles performed many miraculous signs and wonders.
NLT.44: And all the believers met together in one place and shared everything they had.
NLT.45: They sold their property and possessions and shared the money with those in need.
NLT.46: They worshiped together at the Temple each day, met in homes for the Lord’s Supper, and shared their meals with great joy and generosity —
NLT.47: all the while praising God and enjoying the goodwill of all the people. And each day the Lord added to their fellowship those who were being saved.

WEB.1: Now when the day of Pentecost had come, they were all with one accord in one place.
WEB.2: Suddenly there came from the sky a sound like the rushing of a mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting.
WEB.3: Tongues like fire appeared and were distributed to them, and one sat on each of them.
WEB.4: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit, and began to speak with other languages, as the Spirit gave them the ability to speak.
WEB.5: Now there were dwelling in Jerusalem Jews, devout men, from every nation under the sky.
WEB.6: When this sound was heard, the multitude came together, and were bewildered, because everyone heard them speaking in his own language.
WEB.7: They were all amazed and marveled, saying to one another, “Behold, aren’t all these who speak Galileans?
WEB.8: How do we hear, everyone in our own native language?
WEB.9: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, and people from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
WEB.10: Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, the parts of Libya around Cyrene, visitors from Rome, both Jews and proselytes,
WEB.11: Cretans and Arabians: we hear them speaking in our languages the mighty works of God!”
WEB.12: They were all amazed, and were perplexed, saying to one another, “What does this mean?”
WEB.13: Others, mocking, said, “They are filled with new wine.”
WEB.14: But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice, and spoke out to them, “You men of Judea, and all you who dwell at Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and listen to my words.
WEB.15: For these aren’t drunken, as you suppose, seeing it is only the third hour of the day.
WEB.16: But this is what has been spoken through the prophet Joel:
WEB.17: ‘It will be in the last days, says God, that I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh. Your sons and your daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions. Your old men will dream dreams.
WEB.18: Yes, and on my servants and on my handmaidens in those days, I will pour out my Spirit, and they will prophesy.
WEB.19: I will show wonders in the sky above, and signs on the earth beneath; blood, and fire, and billows of smoke.
WEB.20: The sun will be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and glorious day of the Lord comes.
WEB.21: It will be that whoever will call on the name of the Lord will be saved.’
WEB.22: “Men of Israel, hear these words! Jesus of Nazareth, a man approved by God to you by mighty works and wonders and signs which God did by him among you, even as you yourselves know,
WEB.23: him, being delivered up by the determined counsel and foreknowledge of God, you have taken by the hand of lawless men, crucified and killed;
WEB.24: whom God raised up, having freed him from the agony of death, because it was not possible that he should be held by it.
WEB.25: For David says concerning him, ‘I saw the Lord always before my face, For he is on my right hand, that I should not be moved.
WEB.26: Therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover my flesh also will dwell in hope;
WEB.27: because you will not leave my soul in Hades,  neither will you allow your Holy One to see decay.
WEB.28: You made known to me the ways of life. You will make me full of gladness with your presence.’
WEB.29: “Brothers, I may tell you freely of the patriarch David, that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.
WEB.30: Therefore, being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him that of the fruit of his body, according to the flesh, he would raise up the Christ to sit on his throne,
WEB.31: he foreseeing this spoke about the resurrection of the Christ, that neither was his soul left in Hades,  nor did his flesh see decay.
WEB.32: This Jesus God raised up, to which we all are witnesses.
WEB.33: Being therefore exalted by the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this, which you now see and hear.
WEB.34: For David didn’t ascend into the heavens, but he says himself, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit by my right hand,
WEB.35: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.”’
WEB.36: “Let all the house of Israel therefore know certainly that God has made him both Lord and Christ, this Jesus whom you crucified.”
WEB.37: Now when they heard this, they were cut to the heart, and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, “Brothers, what shall we do?”
WEB.38: Peter said to them, “Repent, and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
WEB.39: For the promise is to you, and to your children, and to all who are far off, even as many as the Lord our God will call to himself.”
WEB.40: With many other words he testified, and exhorted them, saying, “Save yourselves from this crooked generation!”
WEB.41: Then those who gladly received his word were baptized. There were added that day about three thousand souls.
WEB.42: They continued steadfastly in the apostles’ teaching and fellowship, in the breaking of bread, and prayer.
WEB.43: Fear came on every soul, and many wonders and signs were done through the apostles.
WEB.44: All who believed were together, and had all things in common.
WEB.45: They sold their possessions and goods, and distributed them to all, according as anyone had need.
WEB.46: Day by day, continuing steadfastly with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread at home, they took their food with gladness and singleness of heart,
WEB.47: praising God, and having favor with all the people. The Lord added to the assembly day by day those who were being saved.

AMP.1: When the day of Pentecost had come, they were all together in one place,
AMP.2: and suddenly a sound came from heaven like a rushing violent wind, and it filled the whole house where they were sitting.
AMP.3: There appeared to them tongues resembling fire, which were being distributed [among them], and they rested on each one of them [as each person received the Holy Spirit].
AMP.4: And they were all filled [that is, diffused throughout their being] with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other tongues (different languages), as the Spirit was giving them the ability to speak out [clearly and appropriately].
AMP.5: Now there were Jews living in Jerusalem, devout and God-fearing men from every nation under heaven.
AMP.6: And when this sound was heard, a crowd gathered, and they were bewildered because each one was hearing those in the upper room speaking in his own language or dialect.
AMP.7: They were completely astonished, saying, "Look! Are not all of these who are speaking Galileans?
AMP.8: Then how is it that each of us hears in our own language or native dialect?
AMP.9: [Among us there are] Parthians, Medes and Elamites, and people of Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia [Minor],
AMP.10: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the districts of Libya around Cyrene, and the visitors from Rome, both Jews and proselytes (Gentile converts to Judaism),
AMP.11: Cretans and Arabs—we all hear them speaking in our [native] tongues about the mighty works of God!"
AMP.12: And they were beside themselves with amazement and were greatly perplexed, saying one to another, "What could this mean?"
AMP.13: But others were laughing and joking and ridiculing them, saying, "They are full of sweet wine and are drunk!"
AMP.14: But Peter, standing with the eleven, raised his voice and addressed them: "Men of Judea and all you who live in Jerusalem, let this be explained to you; listen closely and pay attention to what I have to say.
AMP.15: These people are not drunk, as you assume, since it is [only] the third hour of the day (9:00 a.m.);
AMP.16: but this is [the beginning of] what was spoken of through the prophet Joel:
AMP.17:
'And it shall be in the last days,' says God,
'That I will pour out My Spirit upon all mankind;
And your sons and your daughters shall prophesy,
And your young men shall see [divinely prompted] visions,
And your old men shall dream [divinely prompted] dreams;
AMP.18:
Even on My bond-servants, both men and women,
I will in those days pour out My Spirit
And they shall prophesy.
AMP.19:
'And I will bring about wonders in the sky above
And signs (attesting miracles) on the earth below,
Blood and fire and smoking vapor.
AMP.20:
'The sun shall be turned into darkness
And the moon into blood,
Before the great and glorious day of the Lord comes.
AMP.21: 'And it shall be that everyone who calls upon the name of the Lord [invoking, adoring, and worshiping the Lord Jesus] shall be saved (rescued spiritually).'
AMP.22: "Men of Israel, listen to these words: Jesus of Nazareth, a Man accredited and pointed out and attested to you by God with [the power to perform] miracles and wonders and signs which God worked through Him in your [very] midst, just as you yourselves know—
AMP.23: this Man, when handed over [to the Roman authorities] according to the predetermined decision and foreknowledge of God, you nailed to a cross and put to death by the hands of lawless and godless men.
AMP.24: But God raised Him up, releasing Him and bringing an end to the agony of death, since it was impossible for Him to be held in death's power.
AMP.25:
For David says of Him,
'I saw the Lord constantly before me;
For He is at my right hand, so that I will not be shaken [from my state of security].
AMP.26:
'Therefore my heart rejoiced and my tongue exulted exceedingly;
Moreover my flesh also will live in hope [that is, will encamp in anticipation of the resurrection];
AMP.27:
'For You will not forsake me and abandon my soul to Hades (the realm of the dead),
Nor let Your Holy One undergo decay [after death].
AMP.28:
'You have made known to me the ways of life;
You will fill me [infusing my soul] with joy with Your presence.'
AMP.29: "Brothers, I may confidently and freely say to you regarding the patriarch David that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.
AMP.30: And so, being a prophet and knowing fully that God had sworn to him with an oath that He would seat one of his descendants on his throne,
AMP.31: he foresaw and spoke [prophetically] of the resurrection of the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed), that He was not abandoned [in death] to Hades (the realm of the dead), nor did His body undergo decay.
AMP.32: God raised this Jesus [bodily from the dead], and of that [fact] we are all witnesses.
AMP.33: Therefore having been exalted to the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, He has poured out this [blessing] which you both see and hear.
AMP.34:
For David did not ascend into the heavens, yet he himself says,
'The Lord [the Father] said to my LORD [the Son],
"Sit at My right hand,
AMP.35: Until I make Your enemies afootstool for Your feet."'
AMP.36: Therefore let all the house of Israel recognize beyond all doubt that God has made Him both Lord and Christ (Messiah, Anointed)—this Jesus whom you crucified."
AMP.37: Now when they heard this, they were cut to the heart [with remorse and anxiety], and they said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, "Brothers, what are we to do?"
AMP.38: And Peter said to them, "Repent [change your old way of thinking, turn from your sinful ways, accept and follow Jesus as the Messiah] and be baptized, each of you, in the name of Jesus Christ because of the forgiveness of your sins; and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
AMP.39: For the promise [of the Holy Spirit] is for you and your children and for all who are far away [including the Gentiles], as many as the Lord our God calls to Himself."
AMP.40: And Peter solemnly testified and continued to admonish and urge them with many more words, saying, " Be saved from this crooked and unjust generation!"
AMP.41: So then, those who accepted his message were baptized; and on that day about3,000 souls were added [to the body of believers].
AMP.42: They were continually and faithfully devoting themselves to the instruction of the apostles, and to fellowship, to eating meals together and to prayers.
AMP.43: A sense of awe was felt by everyone, and many wonders and signs (attesting miracles) were taking place through the apostles.
AMP.44: And all those who had believed [in Jesus as Savior] were together and had all things in common [considering their possessions to belong to the group as a whole].
AMP.45: And they began selling their property and possessions and were sharing the proceeds with all [the other believers], as anyone had need.
AMP.46: Day after day they met in the temple [area] continuing with one mind, and breaking bread in various private homes. They were eating their meals together with joy and generous hearts,
AMP.47: praising God continually, and having favor with all the people. And the Lord kept adding to their number daily those who were being saved.

MSG.1: When the Feast of Pentecost came, they were all together in one place.
MSG.2: Without warning there was a sound like a strong wind, gale force—no one could tell where it came from. It filled the whole building.
MSG.3: Then, like a wildfire, the Holy Spirit spread through their ranks,
MSG.4: and they started speaking in a number of different languages as the Spirit prompted them.
MSG.5: There were many Jews staying in Jerusalem just then, devout pilgrims from all over the world.
MSG.6: When they heard the sound, they came on the run. Then when they heard, one after another, their own mother tongues being spoken, they were thunderstruck.
MSG.7: They couldn't for the life of them figure out what was going on, and kept saying, "Aren't these all Galileans?
MSG.8: How come we're hearing them talk in our various mother tongues?
MSG.9: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; Visitors from Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,
MSG.10: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of Libya belonging to Cyrene; Immigrants from Rome,
MSG.11: both Jews and proselytes; Even Cretans and Arabs! "They're speaking our languages, describing God's mighty works!"
MSG.12: Their heads were spinning; they couldn't make head or tail of any of it. They talked back and forth, confused: "What's going on here?"
MSG.13: Others joked, "They're drunk on cheap wine."
MSG.14: That's when Peter stood up and, backed by the other eleven, spoke out with bold urgency: "Fellow Jews, all of you who are visiting Jerusalem, listen carefully and get this story straight.
MSG.15: These people aren't drunk as some of you suspect. They haven't had time to get drunk—it's only nine o'clock in the morning.
MSG.16: This is what the prophet Joel announced would happen:
MSG.17: "In the Last Days," God says, "I will pour out my Spirit on every kind of people: Your sons will prophesy, also your daughters; Your young men will see visions, your old men dream dreams.
MSG.18: When the time comes, I'll pour out my Spirit On those who serve me, men and women both, and they'll prophesy.
MSG.19: I'll set wonders in the sky above and signs on the earth below, Blood and fire and billowing smoke,
MSG.20: the sun turning black and the moon blood-red, Before the Day of the Lord arrives, the Day tremendous and marvelous;
MSG.21: And whoever calls out for help to me, God, will be saved."
MSG.22: "Fellow Israelites, listen carefully to these words: Jesus the Nazarene, a man thoroughly accredited by God to you—the miracles and wonders and signs that God did through him are common knowledge—
MSG.23: this Jesus, following the deliberate and well-thought-out plan of God, was betrayed by men who took the law into their own hands, and was handed over to you. And you pinned him to a cross and killed him.
MSG.24: But God untied the death ropes and raised him up. Death was no match for him.
MSG.25: David said it all: I saw God before me for all time. Nothing can shake me; he's right by my side.
MSG.26: I'm glad from the inside out, ecstatic; I've pitched my tent in the land of hope.
MSG.27: I know you'll never dump me in Hades; I'll never even smell the stench of death.
MSG.28: You've got my feet on the life-path, with your face shining sun-joy all around.
MSG.29: "Dear friends, let me be completely frank with you. Our ancestor David is dead and buried—his tomb is in plain sight today.
MSG.30: But being also a prophet and knowing that God had solemnly sworn that a descendant of his would rule his kingdom,
MSG.31: seeing far ahead, he talked of the resurrection of the Messiah—'no trip to Hades, no stench of death.'
MSG.32: This Jesus, God raised up. And every one of us here is a witness to it.
MSG.33: Then, raised to the heights at the right hand of God and receiving the promise of the Holy Spirit from the Father, he poured out the Spirit he had just received. That is what you see and hear.
MSG.34: For David himself did not ascend to heaven, but he did say, God said to my Master, "Sit at my right hand
MSG.35: Until I make your enemies a stool for resting your feet."
MSG.36: "All Israel, then, know this: There's no longer room for doubt—God made him Master and Messiah, this Jesus whom you killed on a cross."
MSG.37: Cut to the quick, those who were there listening asked Peter and the other apostles, "Brothers! Brothers! So now what do we do?"
MSG.38: Peter said, "Change your life. Turn to God and be baptized, each of you, in the name of Jesus Christ, so your sins are forgiven. Receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
MSG.39: The promise is targeted to you and your children, but also to all who are far away—whomever, in fact, our Master God invites."
MSG.40: He went on in this vein for a long time, urging them over and over, "Get out while you can; get out of this sick and stupid culture!"
MSG.41: That day about three thousand took him at his word, were baptized and were signed up.
MSG.42: They committed themselves to the teaching of the apostles, the life together, the common meal, and the prayers.
MSG.43: Everyone around was in awe—all those wonders and signs done through the apostles!
MSG.44: And all the believers lived in a wonderful harmony, holding everything in common.
MSG.45: They sold whatever they owned and pooled their resources so that each person's need was met.
MSG.46: They followed a daily discipline of worship in the Temple followed by meals at home, every meal a celebration, exuberant and joyful,
MSG.47: as they praised God. People in general liked what they saw. Every day their number grew as God added those who were saved.

NET.9: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, and residents of Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and the province of Asia,
NET.10: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of Libya near Cyrene, and visitors from Rome,
NET.11: both Jews and proselytes, Cretans and Arabs — we hear them speaking in our own languages about the great deeds God has done!”
NET.12: All were astounded and greatly confused, saying to one another, “What does this mean?”
NET.13: But others jeered at the speakers, saying, “They are drunk on new wine!”
NET.14: But Peter stood up with the eleven, raised his voice, and addressed them: “You men of Judea and all you who live in Jerusalem, know this and listen carefully to what I say.
NET.15: In spite of what you think, these men are not drunk, for it is only nine o’clock in the morning.
NET.16: But this is what was spoken about through the prophet Joel:
NET.17: ‘And in the last days it will be,’ God says, ‘that I will pour out my Spirit on all people, and your sons and your daughters will prophesy, and your young men will see visions, and your old men will dream dreams.
NET.18: Even on my servants, both men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy.
NET.19: And I will perform wonders in the sky above and miraculous signs on the earth below, blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
NET.20: The sun will be changed to darkness and the moon to blood before the great and glorious day of the Lord comes.
NET.21: And then everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.’
NET.22: “Men of Israel, listen to these words: Jesus the Nazarene, a man clearly attested to you by God with powerful deeds, wonders, and miraculous signs that God performed among you through him, just as you yourselves know —
NET.23: this man, who was handed over by the predetermined plan and foreknowledge of God, you executed by nailing him to a cross at the hands of Gentiles.
NET.24: But God raised him up, having released him from the pains of death, because it was not possible for him to be held in its power.
NET.25: For David says about him, ‘I saw the Lord always in front of me, for he is at my right hand so that I will not be shaken.
NET.26: Therefore my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced; my body also will live in hope,
NET.27: because you will not leave my soul in Hades, nor permit your Holy One to experience decay.
NET.28: You have made known to me the paths of life; you will make me full of joy with your presence.’
NET.29: “Brothers, I can speak confidently to you about our forefather David, that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.
NET.30: So then, because he was a prophet and knew that God had sworn to him with an oath to seat one of his descendants on his throne,
NET.31: David by foreseeing this spoke about the resurrection of the Christ, that he was neither abandoned to Hades, nor did his body experience decay.
NET.32: This Jesus God raised up, and we are all witnesses of it.
NET.33: So then, exalted to the right hand of God, and having received the promise of the Holy Spirit from the Father, he has poured out what you both see and hear.
NET.34: For David did not ascend into heaven, but he himself says, ‘The Lord said to my lord, “Sit at my right hand
NET.35: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.”’
NET.36: Therefore let all the house of Israel know beyond a doubt that God has made this Jesus whom you crucified both Lord and Christ.”
NET.37: Now when they heard this, they were acutely distressed and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, “What should we do, brothers?”
NET.38: Peter said to them, “Repent, and each one of you be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
NET.39: For the promise is for you and your children, and for all who are far away, as many as the Lord our God will call to himself.”
NET.40: With many other words he testified and exhorted them saying, “Save yourselves from this perverse generation!”
NET.41: So those who accepted his message were baptized, and that day about three thousand people were added.
NET.42: They were devoting themselves to the apostles’ teaching and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer.
NET.43: Reverential awe came over everyone, and many wonders and miraculous signs came about by the apostles.
NET.44: All who believed were together and held everything in common,
NET.45: and they began selling their property and possessions and distributing the proceeds to everyone, as anyone had need.
NET.46: Every day they continued to gather together by common consent in the temple courts, breaking bread from house to house, sharing their food with glad and humble hearts,
NET.47: praising God and having the good will of all the people. And the Lord was adding to their number every day those who were being saved.
